NWS damage survey confirms a microburst near Monterey with peak wind speeds of 85 mph. Multiple properties damaged, including collapse of wooden garage, major loss of a metal and wooden barn, major loss of metal roofing on another barn, and a hole from a 2-inch diameter branch that punctured through two doors of a house. Multiple small holes on siding of a house, from copious amounts of wind-driven hail of at least one inch in diameter. The focused microburst ended around 6:22 PM EDT, though additional pockets of damage continued to the east.

A thunderstorm moved through parts of the Town of Hancock in Delaware County, NY, roughly 2 miles west of Fishs Eddy just before 5PM on Tuesday, May 6, 2025. This thunderstorm produced a weak microburst, which resulted in uprooted and some snapped narrow trees along its path. Some of the uprooted trees may have also been aided by saturated soil conditions.

*** 3 FATAL *** NWS storm survey confirmed EF1 tornado with maximum winds of 105 mph spun up in a line of severe thunderstorms. Numerous trees uprooted or snapped, many of them on structures. 3 homes were sliced or crushed by trees. 2 residents were killed in one of them, and a third fatality occurred on the crushed end of another residence.

Rotation in a kink in the line of severe storms generated an EF1 tornado in a 4.74 mile path from just south of Westmoreland, through the western edge of the Hamilton College Campus, to near Clinton in Oneida County NY. Numerous trees were uprooted or snapped, and a farm sustained significant damage to its silo and outbuildings. Debris from the farm was lofted at least 1000 feet across the field, with some of it impaling the ground as debris missiles. It lifted after about 11 minutes.

An exceptional swath of hail damage, approximately 2.5 miles wide and 5 miles long, decimated the landscape. Many trees were completely stripped of their foliage, including an entire hillside, which bore the brunt of the storm and appeared entirely bare. Widespread hail damage was also reported to vehicles and homes within the affected area. Spotters reported golf ball sized hail. The hail accumulated in some areas making the ground white and roads slippery.

Widespread tree damage that was consistent with 65 to 85 mph winds occurred throughout the Oakland and Susquehanna Depot, PA area due to a long tracking supercell that caused a large downburst. There were enhanced areas of tree damage where local terrain effects caused funneling of winds and eddies to form. The winds were noticeably higher in these areas, peaking at 90 to 100 mph. Some locations where these more intense funneling winds occurred were along Oak Hill Road and Skinner Hill Road in Oakland and in several drainages aligning from northwest to southeast with the storm motion. Also, near Boyden Street in Oakland there were several soft and hardwood trees snapped 20 feet above the trunk. Finally, along Turnpike Street in Susquehanna Depot, the cemetery had several large pine trees snapped 15 feet above the trunk and a large maple that was snapped. The damage in these areas showed signs of rotation likely was caused from winds interacting with the terrain and localized wind speeds up to 100 mph.

Several barns damaged and one destroyed along River Road. A few trees on houses and cars. Siding torn off of one house, garage damage for another. Additional damage on Grimes Road including gas station. Photos sent to NWS via social media. Estimated 70-80 mph winds. Radar-estimated time.

A tent collapsed outside of Gate 4 of the Great New York State Fair and brought down a utility wire onto Sate Fair Boulevard. An awning of another stand was damaged as well. Time estimated by radar.

*** 1 INJ *** Update to previous tstm wnd dmg report. Large tent collapsed outside of Gate 4 of the Great New York State Fair and brought down a utility wire onto State Fair Boulevard. The storm collapsed a large jumbotron, while also downing trees in a part of the fairgrounds. Dozens of instances of mostly minor damage from fair vendors, though some heavily damaged tents or awnings. Someone was injured when a rope-tethered plank of wood got caught by the wind and struck them.

A thunderstorm moved through far Northern Oneida County around 6 PM on September 4th. It had broad rotation that tightened up as it approached Ava NY. A weak tornado touched down just before 6 pm and traveled 3 miles and on the ground for approximately 8 minutes.
Large Tree Down. Time estimated from radar.
Photo of tree down on a house on Flint Town Road. Time and exact location estimated by radar.

Just before noon, a storm along a warm front separated from the rest of the line and began to rotate. With a favorable environment but low instability, a weak tornado touched down near Callicoon Center. With near peak foliage in parts of the Catskills, it was easy for the weak circulation to loft leaves into the storm that were detected by radar with a debris signature. The circulation was strongest on hilltops and weak to non existent in valleys.
